description_long: This is a hand tool featuring a robust wooden handle and a metallic head with two distinct ends. The head is made of steel and has a pointed pick on one side and a flat chisel-like blade on the other, making it suitable for breaking, prying, or chipping hard surfaces. The handle is cylindrical, straight, and appears to be made from a single piece of wood, providing a comfortable grip for manual use. The metal head is securely fitted onto the handle, with no visible fasteners or additional attachments. The tool shows a natural wood grain and a slightly weathered metallic finish, suggesting it is built for durability in demanding environments. This type of hammer is commonly used in construction, landscaping, or urban maintenance for tasks such as breaking concrete or prying up materials.
description: A sturdy hand tool with a wooden handle and a dual-ended steel head, featuring a pointed pick and a flat chisel blade. Designed for breaking or prying hard surfaces in urban environments.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and both ends of the steel head: the pointed pick and the flat chisel blade.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the width of the steel head and the circular cross-section of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the handle and both ends of the head, with the pick and chisel blade reversed from view 0.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the handle's end and the underside of the steel head, with the pick and chisel blade visible from below.
attribute_handle_type: wooden straight handle, cylindrical, smooth with visible wood grain, no additional grip enhancements
attribute_head_type: dual-ended steel head with a pointed pick on one side and a flat chisel blade on the other, typical of a pick hammer or mattock
